votes.rationale: The revise command works correctly across all tested paths: default 3-file scope, narrow --files selection, and all five error cases (disallowed file, missing motion, missing notes, missing directory, missing API key). Atomic write behavior confirmed — API failure or incomplete response leaves existing files untouched. Structural governance files are never modified.

Revision is correctly narrower than draft: smaller default scope, allowed-file guard, all-or-nothing write semantics. The motion.yaml structural field preservation via mergeMotionYamlNarrative is well-designed. The hard ban on evidence content is enforced in the system prompt. Minor stdout inconsistency in the first test run is cosmetic and does not affect file-write correctness.

Motion Factory v0 now supports the full draft-review-revise cycle: context gathering, deterministic scaffolding, model-assisted narrative generation, and selective narrative revision. The factory remains a bounded drafting assistant with no governance authority. Human review before council:run is the governance boundary.
